tools.name: egfr_calculator
tools.description: Calculates estimated Glomerular Filtration Rate (eGFR) using CKD-EPI formula. Used to assess kidney function and determine appropriate drug dosing.

output.description: eGFR value and CKD stage classification

tools.clinical_notes: eGFR < 60 mL/min/1.73m² indicates CKD. Drug dosing adjustments required for eGFR < 30.

tools.name: drug_dosing_calculator
tools.description: Calculates appropriate drug doses based on patient parameters (weight, renal function, hepatic function). Supports dose adjustments for special populations.

parameters.name: egfr
parameters.description: eGFR value for renal dose adjustment

tools.clinical_notes: Always verify calculated doses with clinical guidelines. Consider patient-specific factors.

tools.name: drug_interaction_checker
tools.description: Checks for potential drug-drug interactions and provides severity assessment and management recommendations.

parameters.name: drug_list
parameters.description: List of medications to check for interactions

tools.clinical_notes: Severity levels: Major (avoid combination), Moderate (monitor), Minor (no action needed).

tools.name: vital_signs_analyzer
tools.description: Analyzes vital signs (BP, HR, RR, Temp, SpO2) and identifies abnormalities, trends, and critical values requiring immediate attention.

output.description: Analysis of vital signs with alerts

tools.clinical_notes: Critical values: SBP > 180 or < 90, HR > 120 or < 50, Temp > 40 or < 35, SpO2 < 90%

tools.name: lab_values_interpreter
tools.description: Interprets common laboratory values and identifies abnormalities, trends, and clinical significance. Includes CBC, CMP, coagulation studies, and cardiac markers.
tools.clinical_notes: Always interpret lab values in clinical context. Consider patient demographics and comorbidities.

tools.name: clinical_calculator
tools.description: Multi-purpose clinical calculator including BMI, risk scores, decision rules, and diagnostic criteria. Supports FRAX, CHA2DS2-VASc, HAS-BLED, Wells criteria, etc.
tools.clinical_notes: Use clinical scores as aids to decision-making, not replacements for clinical judgment.

tools.name: fhir_get_condition
tools.description:
Condition.Search (Problems) This web service retrieves problems from a patient's chart. This includes any data found in the patient's problem list across all encounters. This resource can be queried by a combination of patient ID and status.

Note that this resource retrieves only data stored in problem list records. As a result, medical history data documented outside of a patient's problem list isn't available to applications using this service unless that data is retrieved using another method.

This resource does not return unconfirmed Condition resources in the "holding tank" that drives the EpicCare Reconcile Outside Data Activity. Note - once a clinician reconciles a problem, a new Condition resource associated with the reconciled problem will be available in the normal Condition.Search results.

parameters.name: patient
parameters.description: Reference to a patient resource the condition is for.

original_schema.name: GET {api_base}/Condition
original_schema.description:

Note that this resource retrieves only data stored in problem list records. As a result, medical history data documented outside of a patient's problem list isn't available to applications using this service unless that data is retrieved using another method.

This resource does not return unconfirmed Condition resources in the "holding tank" that drives the EpicCare Reconcile Outside Data Activity. Note - once a clinician reconciles a problem, a new Condition resource associated with the reconciled problem will be available in the normal Condition.Search results.

patient.description: Reference to a patient resource the condition is for.

tools.name: fhir_get_observation
tools.description: Observation.Search (Labs) The Observation (Labs) resource returns component level data for lab results.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date when the specimen was obtained.

parameters.name: patient
parameters.description: Reference to a patient resource the condition is for.

original_schema.name: GET {api_base}/Observation
original_schema.description: Observation.Search (Labs) The Observation (Labs) resource returns component level data for lab results.

date.description: Date when the specimen was obtained.

patient.description: Reference to a patient resource the condition is for.

tools.name: fhir_get_observation
tools.description:
Observation.Search (Vitals) This web service will retrieve vital sign data from a patient's chart, as well as any other non-duplicable data found in the patient's flowsheets across all encounters.

This resource requires the use of encoded flowsheet IDs. Work with each organization to obtain encoded flowsheet IDs. Note that encoded flowsheet IDs will be different for each organization. Encoded flowsheet IDs are also different across production and non-production environments.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description: Use "vital-signs" to search for vitals observations.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The date range for when the observation was taken.

parameters.name: patient
parameters.description: Reference to a patient resource the condition is for.

original_schema.name: GET {api_base}/Observation
original_schema.description:
Observation.Search (Vitals) This web service will retrieve vital sign data from a patient's chart, as well as any other non-duplicable data found in the patient's flowsheets across all encounters.

This resource requires the use of encoded flowsheet IDs. Work with each organization to obtain encoded flowsheet IDs. Note that encoded flowsheet IDs will be different for each organization. Encoded flowsheet IDs are also different across production and non-production environments.

category.description: Use "vital-signs" to search for vitals observations.

date.description: The date range for when the observation was taken.

patient.description: Reference to a patient resource the condition is for.

tools.name: fhir_post_observation
tools.description: Observation.Create (Vitals) The FHIR Observation.Create (Vitals) resource can file to all non-duplicable flowsheet rows, including vital signs. This resource can file vital signs for all flowsheets.

parameters.name: effectiveDateTime
parameters.description: The date and time the observation was taken, in ISO format.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The status of the observation. Only a value of "final" is supported. We do not support filing data that isn't finalized.

original_schema.name: POST {api_base}/Observation
original_schema.description: Observation.Create (Vitals) The FHIR Observation.Create (Vitals) resource can file to all non-duplicable flowsheet rows, including vital signs. This resource can file vital signs for all flowsheets.

text.description: The flowsheet ID, encoded flowsheet ID, or LOINC codes to flowsheet mapping. What is being measured.

effectiveDateTime.description: The date and time the observation was taken, in ISO format.

status.description: The status of the observation. Only a value of "final" is supported. We do not support filing data that isn't finalized.

reference.description: The patient FHIR ID for whom the observation is about.

tools.name: fhir_get_medicationrequest
tools.description:
MedicationRequest.Search (Signed Medication Order) You can use the search interaction to query for medication orders based on a patient and optionally status or category.

This resource can return various types of medications, including inpatient-ordered medications, clinic-administered medications (CAMS), patient-reported medications, and reconciled medications from Care Everywhere and other external sources.

The R4 version of this resource also returns patient-reported medications. Previously, patient-reported medications were not returned by the STU3 version of MedicationRequest and needed to be queried using the STU3 MedicationStatement resource. This is no longer the case. The R4 version of this resource returns patient-reported medications with the reportedBoolean element set to True. If the informant is known, it is also specified in the reportedReference element.

parameters.name: category
parameters.description:
The category of medication orders to search for. By default all categories are searched.

Supported categories:
Inpatient
Outpatient (those administered in the clinic - CAMS)
Community (prescriptions)
Discharge

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: The medication administration date. This parameter corresponds to the dosageInstruction.timing.repeat.boundsPeriod element. Medication orders that do not have start and end dates within the search parameter dates are filtered. If the environment supports multiple time zones, the search dates are adjusted one day in both directions, so more medications might be returned than expected. Use caution when filtering a medication list by date as it is possible to filter out important active medications. Starting in the November 2022 version of Epic, this parameter is respected. In May 2022 and earlier versions of Epic, this parameter is allowed but is ignored and no date filtering is applied.

original_schema.name: GET {api_base}/MedicationRequest
original_schema.description:
MedicationRequest.Search (Signed Medication Order) You can use the search interaction to query for medication orders based on a patient and optionally status or category.

This resource can return various types of medications, including inpatient-ordered medications, clinic-administered medications (CAMS), patient-reported medications, and reconciled medications from Care Everywhere and other external sources.

The R4 version of this resource also returns patient-reported medications. Previously, patient-reported medications were not returned by the STU3 version of MedicationRequest and needed to be queried using the STU3 MedicationStatement resource. This is no longer the case. The R4 version of this resource returns patient-reported medications with the reportedBoolean element set to True. If the informant is known, it is also specified in the reportedReference element.

category.description:
The category of medication orders to search for. By default all categories are searched.

Supported categories:
Inpatient
Outpatient (those administered in the clinic - CAMS)
Community (prescriptions)
Discharge

date.description: The medication administration date. This parameter corresponds to the dosageInstruction.timing.repeat.boundsPeriod element. Medication orders that do not have start and end dates within the search parameter dates are filtered. If the environment supports multiple time zones, the search dates are adjusted one day in both directions, so more medications might be returned than expected. Use caution when filtering a medication list by date as it is possible to filter out important active medications. Starting in the November 2022 version of Epic, this parameter is respected. In May 2022 and earlier versions of Epic, this parameter is allowed but is ignored and no date filtering is applied.

parameters.name: authoredOn
parameters.description: The date the prescription was written.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The status of the medication request. Use "active"

text.description: The order display name of the medication, otherwise the record name.

authoredOn.description: The date the prescription was written.

unit.description: unit for the dose such as "g"
unit for the rate such as "h"

status.description: The status of the medication request. Use "active"

reference.description: The patient FHIR ID for who the medication request is for.

tools.name: fhir_get_procedure
tools.description:
Procedure.Search (Orders) The FHIR Procedure resource defines an activity performed on or with a patient as part of the provision of care. It corresponds with surgeries and procedures performed, including endoscopies and biopsies, as well as less invasive actions like counseling and physiotherapy.

This resource is designed for a high-level summarization around the occurrence of a procedure, and not for specific procedure log documentation - a concept that does not yet have a defined FHIR Resource. When searching, only completed procedures are returned.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: External CPT codes associated with the procedure.

parameters.name: date
parameters.description: Date or period that the procedure was performed, using the FHIR date parameter format.

parameters.name: patient
parameters.description: Reference to a patient resource the condition is for.

original_schema.name: GET {api_base}/Procedure
original_schema.description:
Procedure.Search (Orders) The FHIR Procedure resource defines an activity performed on or with a patient as part of the provision of care. It corresponds with surgeries and procedures performed, including endoscopies and biopsies, as well as less invasive actions like counseling and physiotherapy.

This resource is designed for a high-level summarization around the occurrence of a procedure, and not for specific procedure log documentation - a concept that does not yet have a defined FHIR Resource. When searching, only completed procedures are returned.

code.description: External CPT codes associated with the procedure.

date.description: Date or period that the procedure was performed, using the FHIR date parameter format.

patient.description: Reference to a patient resource the condition is for.

parameters.name: code
parameters.description: The standard terminology codes mapped to the procedure, which can include LOINC, SNOMED, CPT, CBV, THL, or Kuntalitto codes.

parameters.name: authoredOn
parameters.description: The order instant. This is the date and time of when an order is signed or signed and held.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: The status of the service request. Use "active"

parameters.name: occurrenceDateTime
parameters.description: The date and time for the service request to be conducted, in ISO format.

code.description: The standard terminology codes mapped to the procedure, which can include LOINC, SNOMED, CPT, CBV, THL, or Kuntalitto codes.

authoredOn.description: The order instant. This is the date and time of when an order is signed or signed and held.

status.description: The status of the service request. Use "active"

reference.description: The patient FHIR ID for who the service request is for.

occurrenceDateTime.description: The date and time for the service request to be conducted, in ISO format.

tools.name: fhir_get_patient
tools.description: Patient.Search This web service allows filtering or searching for patients based on a number of parameters, and retrieves patient demographic information from a patient's chart for each matching patient record. This service also does not respect the same filtering as MyChart, with the exception of the careProvider parameter.

parameters.name: address-city
parameters.description: The city for patient's home address.

parameters.name: address-postalcode
parameters.description: The postal code for patient's home address.

parameters.name: address-state
parameters.description: The state for the patient's home address.

parameters.name: birthdate
parameters.description: The patient's date of birth in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

parameters.name: gender
parameters.description: The patient's legal sex. Starting in the August 2021 version of Epic, the legal-sex parameter is preferred.

parameters.name: given
parameters.description: The patient's given name. May include first and middle names.

parameters.name: legal-sex
parameters.description: The patient’s legal sex. Takes precedence over the gender search parameter. Available starting in the August 2021 version of Epic.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Any part of the patient's name. When discrete name parameters are used, such as family or given, this parameter is ignored.

parameters.name: telecom
parameters.description: The patient's phone number or email.

original_schema.name: GET {api_base}/Patient
original_schema.description: Patient.Search This web service allows filtering or searching for patients based on a number of parameters, and retrieves patient demographic information from a patient's chart for each matching patient record. This service also does not respect the same filtering as MyChart, with the exception of the careProvider parameter.

address-city.description: The city for patient's home address.

address-postalcode.description: The postal code for patient's home address.

address-state.description: The state for the patient's home address.

birthdate.description: The patient's date of birth in the format YYYY-MM-DD.

gender.description: The patient's legal sex. Starting in the August 2021 version of Epic, the legal-sex parameter is preferred.

given.description: The patient's given name. May include first and middle names.

legal-sex.description: The patient’s legal sex. Takes precedence over the gender search parameter. Available starting in the August 2021 version of Epic.

name.description: Any part of the patient's name. When discrete name parameters are used, such as family or given, this parameter is ignored.

telecom.description: The patient's phone number or email.
